title: Senior Director, Enterprise Applications
raw_jd:
Company logo for, Mirion Technologies.

Mirion Technologies

Senior Director, Enterprise Applications
About the job

The Senior Director, Enterprise Applications is accountable for defining and executing the global enterprise applications strategy, ensuring alignment with business priorities and long-term growth objectives. Reporting to the CIO, this role provides leadership across a diverse applications portfolio, including ERP, CRM, and data platforms, while guiding senior leaders and global teams through complex transformation initiatives.

A core mandate of this role is to lead and refine the enterprise applications consolidation and modernization strategy—validating roadmaps, supporting product selection, sequencing global deployments, and balancing near-term optimization with long-term investment. This includes ERP as well as adjacent and enabling platforms across the enterprise applications landscape.

This leader partners closely with executive stakeholders to drive disciplined prioritization, measurable outcomes, and strong adoption across regions. In parallel, the role maintains operational accountability for the current enterprise applications ecosystem—ensuring performance against KPIs, SDLC rigor, and business continuity across ERP (e.g., SAP, D365, Infor) and other core platforms such as CRM, Marketing systems, Finance systems and analytics/data platforms.

The ideal candidate brings deep expertise in ERP systems—particularly within Supply Chain and Financials—along with a mastery of SDLC methodologies and a proven track record of leading enterprise-wide implementations.

This is a rare opportunity to shape the future of our enterprise technology landscape, improve operational efficiency, and support scalable growth—all while working at the intersection of strategy, innovation, and execution.

The Senior Director may have direct supervisory responsibility for a team, including Director-level roles.

Occasional travel of up to 30% may be required.

Key Responsibilities

    Define and execute the end‑to‑end enterprise applications strategy (current and future state), including roadmap, implementation, optimization, and lifecycle management across ERP and other core platforms.
    Lead global enterprise application implementations and enhancements, ensuring on‑time delivery, budget discipline, quality outcomes, and strong stakeholder engagement.
    Oversee the stability, performance, security, and operational support of the enterprise applications ecosystem, including upgrades, integrations, vendor management, and incident/problem management.
    Provide strategic and operational leadership for non‑ERP enterprise platforms (e.g., CRM, Marketing, Finance, Analytics, HR, Legal), ensuring fit‑for‑purpose capability, process alignment, and a scalable global support model.
    Own the enterprise application architecture and integration strategy (APIs, iPaaS, master data, identity/access), enabling reliable end‑to‑end processes and a cohesive user experience across the application landscape.
    Provide architectural leadership to ensure scalable, integrated solutions aligned with business priorities and long‑term growth.
    Partner with senior business leaders as a trusted advisor to identify opportunities, drive adoption, and deliver measurable business value; innovate on and identify opportunities and educate the business where modern technology solutions can add value or achieve stated goals.
    Build and lead a high‑performing, global Enterprise Applications organization, including talent development, performance management, and budgeting.
    Drive process improvement, automation, and data‑driven decision‑making through improved analytics, reporting, and governance.
    Establish strong governance, compliance, and SDLC disciplines while fostering effective collaboration between IT and business teams.

Qualifications And Experience

    Experience: 15+ years of IT experience, including 8+ years in senior IT leadership roles with strong people management responsibility in complex, global environments.
    Education: Bachelor’s degree in IT, Engineering, Finance, Supply Chain, or related field required; Master’s degree or MBA preferred.
    Transformation Leadership: Proven success leading large-scale enterprise applications transformations (ERP and non‑ERP) in dynamic, multi-region organizations, including process standardization, change management, and adoption.
    Enterprise Applications Expertise: Deep experience leading, implementing, and supporting enterprise application platforms through full SDLC lifecycles and complex integrations/data migrations; experience with major ERP suites (SAP ECC/S4, D365 F&O, Infor LN) plus adjacent platforms (CRM, HCM, ITSM, EPM, iPaaS) strongly preferred.
    Business & Technical Acumen: Strong understanding of end-to-end business processes across finance, supply chain, HR, and operations, with the ability to translate between business and technical stakeholders.
    Enterprise Platforms: Expertise in system integrations, cloud-based enterprise platforms, and adjacent systems such as Salesforce, ServiceNow, and modern data platforms (Microsoft Fabric, Databricks preferred).
    Leadership & Influence: Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams, influence senior leaders, drive organizational change, and serve as a trusted advisor during enterprise transformation.
    Execution & Decision-Making: Proven capability to manage large, complex initiatives, make sound decisions under pressure, and present recommendations to senior leadership.
    Operational Excellence: Track record of driving process improvement, operational efficiency, governance discipline, and high-quality outcomes.
    Communication & Management: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, strong organizational abilities, and the capacity to manage multiple priorities concurrently.
    Languages: Proficiency in French and/or German is preferred.
    Talent Strategy: Demonstrated ability to build, develop, and sustain high-performing teams through effective workforce planning, engagement, and leadership development.

score.skills_rationale: Strong alignment with ERP expertise (SAP/D365), enterprise applications architecture, and data platforms. Candidate's FMBT/ERP experience, financial systems knowledge, and integration work directly match requirements. Missing some specific platforms like Salesforce/ServiceNow but core competencies align well.
score.scope_rationale: Perfect scope match - this is a C-level reporting senior director role requiring 15+ years experience and 8+ years senior IT leadership. Candidate's progression from individual contributor to architect/manager to current senior role with VA aligns perfectly with expectations for leading global enterprise transformations.
score.pay_rationale: No salary range provided, but senior director roles typically range $200K-300K+ which may exceed candidate's W2 target of $160K-220K.
score.raw_analysis: This is an excellent strategic match - a legitimate senior director role requiring exactly the candidate's combination of ERP expertise, financial systems knowledge, and enterprise architecture experience. The scope perfectly matches their career progression and current VA contractor experience with large-scale ERP transformations. While some specific platform gaps exist, the core competencies align strongly. Main concerns are potential salary mismatch and travel requirements.

cover_letters.content:
Dear Hiring Manager,

My 25+ years leading enterprise applications transformations—including my current role architecting the Department of Veterans Affairs' $2B+ FMBT ERP modernization—positions me uniquely for your Senior Director role. I've spent the last three years designing and implementing data pipelines integrating legacy financial systems with enterprise ERP platforms, exactly the kind of complex, multi-system transformation leadership Mirion needs. At Howard University, I drove a multi-million-dollar Workday implementation as one of three cross-functional leaders during executive disruption, stabilizing delivery and ensuring successful deployment across Finance, HR, and IT stakeholders.

My expertise spans the full enterprise applications ecosystem you're modernizing. I've architected ERP integrations, built enterprise data warehouses, and established governance frameworks that improve audit readiness and operational efficiency. At VitalSpring, I delivered BI/analytics solutions that identified process bottlenecks and saved over $70K in six months. Earlier, I architected a 25,000-user enterprise reporting platform at Lowe's and delivered analytics solutions for Fortune 100 clients including Google and Yahoo. This combination of hands-on technical depth and executive-level strategy execution is precisely what's needed to lead global application consolidation while maintaining operational excellence.

What sets me apart is my ability to bridge technical architecture with business outcomes during high-pressure transformations. I've consistently stepped in to resolve complex integration challenges, debug performance issues, and coordinate across finance, acquisition, and IT stakeholders to meet critical delivery timelines. My track record includes building teams, mentoring engineers, and driving disciplined SDLC processes—the operational leadership capabilities essential for managing Director-level roles and global enterprise implementations.

I'm ready to shape Mirion's enterprise technology landscape and drive the measurable outcomes your transformation demands.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

Having spent the past several years architecting and stabilizing enterprise systems through large-scale ERP transformations, I understand the complexity Mirion Technologies faces in consolidating and modernizing your global applications portfolio. Your need for a Senior Director who can balance strategic vision with operational excellence—particularly around ERP consolidation while maintaining business continuity—aligns directly with my experience leading similar initiatives at the Department of Veterans Affairs and Howard University.

At VA's FMBT program, I've been architecting the data and integration backbone for a massive ERP modernization (iFAMS), designing enterprise data pipelines that connect legacy financial systems with modern ERP platforms while ensuring audit-ready data quality. This work mirrors Mirion's challenge of sequencing global deployments while maintaining operational stability. Similarly, at Howard University, I helped stabilize a multi-million-dollar Workday implementation during executive disruption, stepping into a cross-functional leadership role to resolve system integration issues and drive the program to successful deployment. These experiences have taught me how to navigate the dual pressures of transformation and continuity that define enterprise applications leadership.

Your emphasis on architectural leadership particularly resonates with my approach to building resilient, integrated systems. I've designed end-to-end data architectures that span multiple platforms, established governance frameworks that improve data quality and compliance, and built the technical bridges between finance, operations, and IT teams that make enterprise transformations successful. My background spans the full spectrum—from hands-on debugging of complex integration challenges to presenting strategic recommendations to executive stakeholders—which positions me to drive both the immediate operational needs and long-term modernization vision Mirion requires.

I'm drawn to this opportunity because it combines strategic enterprise architecture with the operational discipline necessary to manage global, multi-platform environments. I'd welcome the chance to discuss how my experience leading ERP transformations and building high-performing technical teams can contribute to Mirion's enterprise applications strategy.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my interest in the Senior Director, Enterprise Applications position at Mirion Technologies. With 25+ years of enterprise systems experience and current leadership of the Department of Veterans Affairs' $1B+ Financial Management Business Transformation (FMBT) program, I bring exactly the ERP expertise and transformation leadership you're seeking. My work designing data pipelines and integration architectures for VA's enterprise ERP rollout (iFAMS), combined with my previous role driving Howard University's multi-million-dollar Workday implementation during executive disruption, demonstrates my ability to deliver complex enterprise transformations in high-pressure environments.

My technical foundation spans the core platforms in your stack—ERP systems integration, enterprise data architectures, and cross-platform consolidation strategies—while my leadership experience includes coordinating across finance, IT, and business stakeholders to resolve system issues and meet delivery timelines. At Howard University, I served as one of three cross-functional drivers stabilizing a critical ERP deployment, establishing data governance frameworks, and building enterprise BI architectures that supported executive decision-making. This combination of hands-on technical expertise and strategic program leadership positions me well to define and execute Mirion's enterprise applications consolidation strategy while maintaining operational excellence across your global portfolio.

Sincerely,
Salil Maniktahla

tailored_resume:
SALIL MANIKTAHLA
Springfield, VA  ·  571-215-8218  ·  salil.maniktahla@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/salilmaniktahla

SUMMARY
Senior data and enterprise applications leader with 25+ years of experience driving large-scale ERP transformations, enterprise application strategies, and system integrations across complex, multi-region environments. Currently leading enterprise data architecture and reporting for the Department of Veterans Affairs' Financial Management Business Transformation (FMBT) program—a multi-billion-dollar ERP modernization initiative spanning legacy systems integration, financial consolidation, and executive reporting.

Proven track record of defining and executing enterprise applications strategies, leading cross-functional teams through complex transformations, and delivering measurable business outcomes. Deep expertise in ERP platforms (iFAMS, Workday, legacy financial systems), enterprise integrations, and data platforms, with strong understanding of end-to-end business processes across finance, supply chain, and operations.

CORE CAPABILITIES
Enterprise Applications & ERP Strategy
    • Large-scale ERP implementations and modernization initiatives
    • Enterprise applications consolidation, roadmap development, and lifecycle management
    • ERP platform expertise (iFAMS/Momentum, Workday, legacy financial systems)
    • System integrations, data migrations, and platform optimization

Business Process & Financial Systems
    • Deep understanding of finance, supply chain, and operational processes
    • Financial reconciliation, audit readiness, and regulatory compliance
    • Budget execution, commitments/obligations/expenditures tracking
    • Process standardization and business transformation

Enterprise Architecture & Integration
    • Data pipelines, ETL/ELT, master data management, system integration
    • API development, enterprise data architecture, and data warehousing
    • Cloud platforms and modern data platforms (Azure data services)

Leadership & Transformation
    • Cross-functional technical leadership across Finance, HR, IT, and Operations
    • Change management, stakeholder engagement, and adoption strategies
    • Team development, mentoring, and performance management
    • Executive reporting and trusted advisor relationships

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

Senior Data & Analytics Architect (Contractor)
U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs – FMBT	Sep 2021 – Present
    • Lead enterprise data architecture and reporting workstreams supporting rollout of VA's multi-billion-dollar ERP transformation (iFAMS/Momentum), serving 350,000+ employees across 1,200+ facilities
    • Define and execute end-to-end data strategy for enterprise applications ecosystem, including roadmap development, implementation oversight, and lifecycle management across legacy and modernized systems
    • Drive global enterprise application implementations ensuring on-time delivery, budget discipline, and stakeholder engagement across finance, acquisition, and operations
    • Oversee stability, performance, and operational support of enterprise data platforms, including system upgrades, integrations, vendor management, and incident resolution
    • Built enterprise reporting systems and executive dashboards supporting CFO-level financial operations and decision-making
    • Established data validation, reconciliation processes, and governance frameworks improving audit readiness and regulatory compliance
    • Provide architectural leadership ensuring scalable, integrated solutions aligned with business priorities and long-term growth objectives

Business Intelligence Analyst – Finance
Howard University	Apr 2020 – Jul 2024
    • Served as key transformation leader during multi-million-dollar Workday ERP implementation, providing strategic and operational leadership across Finance, HR, and IT during period of executive disruption (CIO departure, CFO loss)
    • Led enterprise applications consolidation and modernization strategy, validating roadmaps and supporting product selection for ERP and adjacent platforms
    • Defined and executed enterprise data governance initiative, establishing policies, data quality standards, and validation processes across financial and operational systems
    • Designed and implemented enterprise BI data architecture and data warehouse supporting scalable growth and operational efficiency
    • Served as Workday Analytics Lead in direct collaboration with Deputy CFO, translating between business and technical stakeholders
    • Built high-performing analytics capabilities and KPI frameworks supporting executive decision-making and measurable business outcomes
    • Drove process improvement and automation through improved analytics, reporting, and governance discipline

BI Partner / Strategist (Independent Consultant)
Multiple Clients	Jul 2016 – Mar 2020
    • Led enterprise applications implementations and enhancements across multiple industries, ensuring quality outcomes and strong stakeholder engagement
    • Delivered enterprise application architecture and integration strategies enabling reliable end-to-end processes
    • Provided strategic leadership for analytics and data platforms, ensuring fit-for-purpose capability and scalable global support models
    • Built and mentored high-performing teams across technical and business functions, supporting workforce planning and leadership development
    • Established governance frameworks and SDLC disciplines while fostering effective collaboration between IT and business teams

Co-Founder / CEO & Technical Director
Urban Evolution	Apr 2010 – Apr 2020
    • Served as CEO, CTO, and CIO for multi-location franchise operation, owning enterprise applications strategy, business operations, and technology systems
    • Led enterprise-wide technology implementations supporting scalable business growth across multiple regions
    • Built integrated systems combining financial management, operational analytics, and customer management platforms
    • Demonstrated ability to build, develop, and sustain high-performing teams through effective leadership and organizational development

VitalSpring Technologies
BI Senior Manager	May 2008 – Jul 2016
    • Product Manager for enterprise BI/analytics initiatives across healthcare supply chain organization
    • Led large-scale enterprise applications transformations including process standardization and change management
    • Designed enterprise dashboards and analytics platforms identifying process bottlenecks, delivering $70K+ savings in <6 months
    • Provided strategic and operational leadership ensuring measurable business value and strong adoption across global teams

EARLIER EXPERIENCE
Senior BI Consultant – Claraview (Teradata), 2006–2008
    • Technical architect for 25,000-user enterprise reporting platform (Lowe's); defined enterprise KPIs and analytics frameworks for Fortune 100 client

BI Consultant – Kinetic Networks, 2005–2006
    • Delivered enterprise analytics solutions for Google, Yahoo, Wells Fargo using major BI platforms

IT Specialist – U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, 2002–2005
    • Defined enterprise IT strategy for National Center for Environmental Economics; led team of 6 developers and engineers

Operations Manager – MicroStrategy, 1999–2001
    • Led engineering team delivering integrated enterprise analytics systems; saved ~$312K/year through vendor optimization and operational excellence

SELECTED SYSTEMS & INNOVATION
    • Designed enterprise-grade on-premises LLM/RAG platform for secure knowledge retrieval using local models with role-based access and audit logging
    • Built containerized enterprise environments for data and application services across distributed infrastructure
    • Designed integrated enterprise systems combining data pipelines, event-driven automation, and comprehensive monitoring
    • Operate advanced enterprise lab environment (10GbE backbone, 100TB+ storage, Docker-based services, enterprise-grade routing)

TECHNICAL SKILLS
Enterprise Applications: ERP systems (iFAMS/Momentum, Workday), Financial Systems, Data Platforms
Data & Analytics: SQL, Python, ETL/ELT, Data Modeling, Enterprise Data Pipelines, Master Data Management
BI Platforms: Tableau, Power BI, MicroStrategy
Cloud & Integration: Azure (data services), API development, Enterprise Architecture, System Integrations
Infrastructure: Linux (Ubuntu), Docker, Networking, Distributed Systems, Enterprise Operations
AI/LLM: RAG pipelines, embeddings, vector search, local model deployment

CERTIFICATIONS & EDUCATION
B.A., Washington University in St. Louis
CISSP  ·  Certified Scrum Master  ·  Tableau Certified Professional  ·  MicroStrategy Certified Developer
